channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Two private lunar landers head toward the moon in a roundabout journey
item.description: SpaceX has launched a pair of lunar landers for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jumpstart business up there.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Relief, 21st-century style: As wildfires burn, GoFundMe becomes a repository of harrowing stories
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — They seem endless, these sapping stories of loss. A grandfather starts over in his 90s. A family loses their dream home. People who were already struggling are dealt new, brutal blows.
item.dc:creator: MATT SEDENSKY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: AAPI adults prioritize immigration, but split on mass deportations: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Immigration is an issue that many Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders want the government to focus on this year, but they are sharply divided on what actions President-elect Donald Trump should take when he returns to the White House, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AAPI adults prioritize immigration, but split on mass deportations: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: Immigration is a top issue many Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders want the federal government to focus on this year, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Terry Tang And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A retro blue VW van miraculously survives deadly Los Angeles fire
item.description: Preston Martin figured the retro blue Volkswagen van he slept in for a year during college was a goner, given that he parked it in a Malibu neighborhood just before the Palisades fire ripped through, reducing homes and cars to rubble and charred metal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Octavia Butler imagined LA ravaged by fires. Her Altadena cemetery survived
item.description: ALTADENA, Calif. (AP) — Decades ago, the writer Octavia Butler had imagined a Los Angeles ravaged by fires. The Altadena cemetery where the science fiction and Afrofuturism author is buried did catch fire last week but suffered “minimal damage,” according to a statement on the cemetery's web site.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE and CHRIS PIZZELLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LA family used ‘budget friendly’ system pumping water from pool to help save their home from wildfires
item.description: Homeowners in Palisades Highlands in Los Angeles County showed on social media how they used a pump system with their swimming pool to help save their home from wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth survives attacks from Democrats in testy Senate hearing
item.description: Pete Hegseth on Tuesday battled through a high-stakes Senate hearing in which Democrats sought to paint the Pentagon nominee as inexperienced, unprofessional and toxic due to allegations of sexual abuse and heavy drinking.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Palisades man says wife called 911, fire could've been contained
item.description: A Pacific Palisades resident says the fire that ravaged his neighborhood could have been far less devastating.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Chemicals in sewage sludge fertilizer pose cancer risk, EPA says
item.description: Harmful chemicals in sewage sludge that is spread on pasture land as fertilizer are causing cancer, the Environmental Protection Agency said Tuesday. The risk is highest for people who regularly consume milk, beef and other products from farms where it is spread.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Firefighters deploy, more power is shut off ahead of fierce Southern California winds
item.description: A week after the first fires began, the flames have destroyed thousands of homes and killed at least 24 people.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: Federal prosecutors won't seek charges in deadly arrest of Black motorist Ronald Greene
item.description: Federal prosecutors told family members Tuesday they will not bring charges in the deadly 2019 arrest of Black motorist Ronald Greene, closing the books on a lengthy FBI investigation into the white troopers who stunned, punched and dragged Greene on a roadside and allegations of an attempted cover-
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Flu hospitalizations have doubled in Colorado since late December
item.description: "We took a big jump after Dec. 21, and it's still going up," said Dr. Ned Calonge, chief medical officer at the Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment.
item.dc:creator: Kim Posey, NewsNation

item.title: REAL ID enforcement may be phased, but deadline is the same: What to know
item.description: The REAL ID deadline is fast-approaching and officials want you to be prepared (spoiler: you may already be in compliance).
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: This popular hiking trail may hold clues to how Palisades Fire ignited
item.description: Aerial footage of the area showed more than a dozen investigators riding and walking the area, presumably looking for an ignition point for the Palisades Fire.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: 29 dogs seized in Memphis animal cruelty case
item.description: Police said six dogs were located inside the residence on Staten, and the rest, including eight puppies, were in the yard.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: Historic Powerball winner reportedly loses Malibu home in Palisades Fire
item.description: After winning $2.04 billion on a Powerball ticket in 2022, Edwin Castro reportedly bought multiple Los Angeles-area homes.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, NewsNation

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The Los Angeles County Fire Department has provided maps of the thousands of structures, primarily homes, that were damaged or destroyed in the Palisades and Eaton wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is skipping President-elect Trump's inauguration next week, after raising eyebrows by not attending former President Jimmy Carter's funeral in Washington, D.C., last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Trump claims California had the chance to 'maintain their forests' amid Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: “The problem is it's so dry, it was always so dry there, and it's just … it's just a mess. They could’ve, you know, maintained their forests, and — as I said they have to do,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police hunt 'Haggler After Midnight' mugger targeting certain victims in bizarre crime spree
item.description: The "Haggler After Midnight," who has struck nearly a dozen times in Brooklyn and Queens, forces victims to haggle at knifepoint over what loot they will hand over, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johnson backs conditions on California fire aid
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is backing placing conditions on disaster assistance for California in the wake of the destructive wildfires blazing around the Los Angeles area, a position already drawing opposition from Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Contaminated drinking water is a growing concern for cities facing wildfires
item.description: As fires continue to burn across Los Angeles, several utilities have declared their drinking water unsafe until extensive testing can prove otherwise. A warmer, drier climate means wildfires are getting worse, and encroaching on cities — with devastating impact.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Investigators search landfill site for Michigan mother who vanished 10 days ago
item.description: Police looking for missing Michigan mother-of-two Ashley Elkins are searching through a landfill for evidence in relation to her disappearance. Elkins has not been seen since Jan. 2.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 153 winners of Nobel and World Food prizes seek new ways to grow food to meet surging global need
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — More than 150 recipients of the Nobel and World Food prizes released an open letter Tuesday calling for a dramatic increase in research and a commitment to new food distribution efforts with a goal of producing more crops and avoiding a global hunger crisis in coming decades.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FEMA extends transitional housing program for North Carolina residents displaced by Hurricane Helene
item.description: FEMA is extending its Transitional Sheltering Assistance Program to Jan. 25 for residents of North Carolina who were displaced by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks? It will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: (AP) — If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Anita Bryant, singer known for opposing gay rights, dies at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant, a former Miss Oklahoma, Grammy-nominated singer and prominent booster of orange juice and other products who became known over the second half of her life for her outspoken opposition to gay rights, has died. She was 84.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Patrick and Brittany Mahomes welcome third child
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes and his wife Brittany Mahomes have had a busy bye week
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, KRON4

item.title: Oscars push back nominations announcement amid Calif. wildfires
item.description: With fires still active in the Los Angeles area, the film academy also extended the nominations voting period for its members through Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Photos: Handful of L.A. homes remain untouched amid charred neighborhoods
item.description: The Walsh House, featured in the “Beverly Hills 90210" television series, survived. So did its pristine lawn. Next door, the remains of a neighbor's home was blackened with soot.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Docs: Texas man accused of sending Caitlin Clark violent messages on social media
item.description: A Texas man is accused of sending sexually violent messages from his X account to Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark and then driving to Indianapolis, court documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, WGN 9

item.title: Former Disney World employee to plead guilty to hacking menus, changing allergen information: court records
item.description: A former Walt Disney World employee will plead guilty to hacking menu-creation software to include profanity and alter allergy information, court records indicate.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, ABC 4

item.title: Firm has no plans to salvage more Titanic artifacts, snuffing out legal fight
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — The U.S. government has scrapped its litigation against the company that owns the salvage rights to the Titanic, noting that the firm no longer has expedition plans to the shipwreck that could break federal law.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: List: The 'overused' words banished for 2025 by a Michigan university
item.description: Sorry not sorry, but one Michigan university would appreciate if you did not utilize these words, period.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, KRON4

item.title: New rule could slash nicotine levels in tobacco products
item.description: The Biden administration is poised to try to lower the amount of nicotine in tobacco products, an eleventh-hour effort that’s been years in the making.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Key Republican says he will support Gabbard as Trump DNI pick
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.) said in a Sunday interview that he will support President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii).
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
